challenges.title: Build Your Own wc Tool
challenges.description: Create your own version of the Unix wc (word count) utility. Count bytes, lines, words, and characters in files.

challenges.title: Build Your Own JSON Parser
challenges.description: Build a JSON parser from scratch, learning the fundamentals of lexical analysis and syntactic parsing that underpin compilers and data format processors.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Compression Tool
challenges.description: Build a compression tool using Huffman coding to compress and decompress files.

challenges.title: Build Your Own cut Tool
challenges.description: Build your own version of the Unix cut command for extracting sections from lines of files.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Load Balancer
challenges.description: Build a Layer 7 HTTP load balancer that distributes requests across backend servers using round-robin scheduling with health checks.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Sort Tool
challenges.description: Create your own version of the Unix sort utility, implementing various sorting algorithms and options.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Calculator
challenges.description: Build a calculator that can parse and evaluate mathematical expressions.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Redis Server
challenges.description: Build a lightweight Redis clone - an in-memory data structure server supporting strings, hashes, lists, sets, and sorted sets. Implement the RESP protocol and handle concurrent clients.

challenges.title: Build Your Own grep
challenges.description: Build your own version of grep, the powerful pattern-matching command-line tool.

challenges.title: Build Your Own uniq Tool
challenges.description: Build your own version of the Unix uniq tool for filtering and counting duplicate lines.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Web Server
challenges.description: Build an HTTP web server from scratch that serves files, handles concurrent connections, and includes security measures against directory traversal attacks.

challenges.title: Build Your Own URL Shortener
challenges.description: Build a URL shortening service like Bitly or TinyURL with a REST API, redirect handling, and optional web frontend.

challenges.title: Build Your Own diff Tool
challenges.description: Build your own version of the diff utility to compare files and show differences.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Shell
challenges.description: Build a functional command-line shell with command execution, pipes, built-in commands, signal handling, and command history.

challenges.title: Build Your Own cat Tool
challenges.description: Build your own version of the Unix cat command for concatenating and displaying files.

challenges.title: Build Your Own IRC Client
challenges.description: Build an IRC client that can connect to IRC servers, join channels, and send and receive messages.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Memcached Server
challenges.description: Build a Memcached server - a high-performance distributed memory caching system.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Spotify Client
challenges.description: Build a Spotify client that interacts with the Spotify API to browse and play music.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Discord Bot
challenges.description: Build a Discord bot that responds to commands and interacts with users in a Discord server.

challenges.title: Build Your Own LinkedIn Carousel Generator
challenges.description: Build a tool to generate LinkedIn carousel posts from text content.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Sed
challenges.description: Build your own version of the Unix stream editor sed for text transformation.

challenges.title: Build Your Own DNS Resolver
challenges.description: Build a DNS resolver that translates domain names into IP addresses by implementing the DNS protocol, crafting UDP packets, and performing recursive resolution.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Traceroute
challenges.description: Build your own version of the traceroute network diagnostic tool.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Realtime Chat Client and Server
challenges.description: Build a real-time chat application with both client and server components.

challenges.title: Build Your Own NATS Message Broker
challenges.description: Build a message-oriented middleware server modelled after NATS with pub/sub messaging, protocol parsing, and concurrent client support.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Git
challenges.description: Build a functional git client from scratch supporting repository initialisation, staging, commits, diffs, and pushing to remote repositories.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Rate Limiter
challenges.description: Build a rate limiter to control the rate of requests to an API or service.

challenges.title: Build Your Own NTP Client
challenges.description: Build a Network Time Protocol client to synchronise time with NTP servers.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Scheduling Automation App
challenges.description: Build an app that automates scheduling using calendar APIs.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Lisp Interpreter
challenges.description: Build a Lisp interpreter from scratch with tokenisation, AST construction, evaluation, a REPL, and script execution.

challenges.title: Build Your Own QR Code Generator
challenges.description: Build a QR code generator that encodes data into QR code images.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Crontab Tool
challenges.description: Build your own version of the crontab scheduling utility.

challenges.title: Build Your Own head
challenges.description: Build your own version of the Unix head command to display the first lines of a file.

challenges.title: Build Your Own jq
challenges.description: Build your own version of jq, the lightweight command-line JSON processor.

challenges.title: Build Your Own Google Keep

challenges.description: Build your own version of the curl command-line tool for making HTTP requests.

challenges.title: Build Your Own RTFM For Me Agent

steps: Visit https://codingchallenges.fyi/challenges/intro/ to browse all available challenges.
Pick a challenge that interests you - beginners should start with the wc Tool or JSON Parser challenges.
Choose your preferred programming language - all challenges are language agnostic.
Follow the step-by-step instructions in the challenge. Each step builds on the previous one.
Share your solution on GitHub and tag John Crickett on Bluesky or LinkedIn.
recommended_first_challenges: Build Your Own wc Tool - a gentle introduction to CLI tools and file processing
Build Your Own JSON Parser - learn parsing fundamentals applicable to many domains
Build Your Own URL Shortener - a classic web application project
Build Your Own Calculator - straightforward parsing and evaluation

troubleshooting.general_advice: If you get stuck on a challenge, try these steps in order before giving up:
steps: Re-read the challenge description and the current step carefully - the requirement is often more specific than it first looks.
Search Google or your preferred search engine for the specific error message, protocol detail, or algorithm you're stuck on.
Ask a friend or colleague - explaining the problem out loud often surfaces the answer.
Visit the Coding Challenges Discord server to ask the community.
Look at example shared solutions at https://github.com/CodingChallengesFYI/sharedsolutions to see how other developers approached the same challenge.

faq.question: What programming language should I use?
faq.answer: All challenges are language agnostic. Use whatever language you're most comfortable with, or use it as an opportunity to learn a new one.

faq.question: How long does each challenge take?
faq.answer: Each challenge is designed to be completable in less than 8 hours, though this varies by challenge complexity and your experience level.

faq.question: Do I need to be an experienced developer?
faq.answer: No. Challenges range from beginner to advanced. Start with a beginner challenge like the wc Tool and work your way up.

faq.question: Is the newsletter free?
faq.answer: Yes, the weekly coding challenge newsletter is free. There is an optional paid subscription at $8/month or $80/year that includes monthly AMA sessions, 20% off courses, and subscriber-only content.

faq.question: Are there courses available?
faq.answer: Yes. There are self-paced courses that walk you through specific challenges, and live courses where John Crickett personally teaches Go and systems programming.

faq.question: Where can I get help if I'm stuck?
faq.answer: First, try Googling the specific problem - most error messages and protocol questions have been answered before. If that doesn't help, ask a friend or colleague to talk it through with you. You can also join the Coding Challenges Discord community to ask other developers, or browse example solutions at https://github.com/CodingChallengesFYI/sharedsolutions to see how others approached the same challenge.
